desc: Launches the standalone Document Controller WPF desktop app from a Revit ribbon button.
summary: Launches the standalone Document Controller WPF desktop app from a Revit ribbon button. The Document Controller is NOT hosted inside Revit — it runs as its own process (DocumentController.WPF.exe) and shares the Supabase backend. The command resolves the exe path from an environment variable override, then the published add-in folder, then known development build-output locations. If an instance is already running, it is brought to the foreground instead of launching a duplicate. Uses ErrorPanel for the three-wire error model and CommandFeedback for user notifications.
usecase: User clicks the "Doc Controller" ribbon button to open the standalone document management desktop app while continuing to work in Revit.
notfor: Hosting the Document Controller inside Revit (it is intentionally a separate process so it can run without Revit and share the web/desktop chat core). Launching from headless automation — requires a Windows desktop session.
precondition: DocumentController.WPF.exe must have been built at least once (dotnet build DocumentController/DocumentController.WPF/DocumentController.WPF.csproj) OR be deployed alongside the MEPBridge add-in in the ApplicationPlugins folder.
postcondition: A DocumentController.WPF window is visible on the desktop. If a prior instance existed, that window is brought to the foreground. If the exe cannot be found, a recoverable error is raised listing every probed path for the user.

preprompts: Launching the Document Controller desktop app...
thinkingsteps: Looking for DocumentController.WPF.exe in known locations...
successprompts: Document Controller was already running — brought to foreground.
